rating.reviewNote: ranked about 1,229 of 5,810 Toronto-area listings; a separate Yelp listing shows 177 reviews

evidence.line: Serves halal fried and rotisserie chicken by its own description; no third-party certifier named in any source found.

rating.reviewNote: ranked about 1,692 of 5,797 listings; reviews split on busy-day service

evidence.line: The Halal Monitoring Authority (HMA) lists this exact outlet on its own certified directory; certificate text was not re-confirmed, so treat this as the certifier's listing, not a hard guarantee.

rating.reviewNote: 1,606 reviews; a Google rating around 4.4 was cited separately

evidence.line: All sausages reported gluten-free and the fries get a dedicated fryer, a documented good practice; not a dedicated gluten-free kitchen, and buns are not gluten-free.

rating.reviewNote: delivery-app ratings tend to skew high; weigh lightly

evidence.line: Corn arepas make most of the menu naturally gluten-free and staff are described as cross-contact aware; explicitly not a dedicated gluten-free facility.
Fully vegan by concept: nothing on the menu contains meat, dairy, or eggs, the cleanest claim in the research pass. Walk runs a few minutes past the usual radius.

rating.reviewNote: ranked about 3,650 of 5,757 listings; closes 19:00 weekend evenings, pre-game only

evidence.line: Sauces reported vegan with tofu and vegetable swaps available; a vegetarian-friendly menu in a shared kitchen, not a vegetarian restaurant.
Sauces reported vegan with tofu and vegetable swaps available; a vegetarian-friendly menu in a shared kitchen, not a vegetarian restaurant.

rating.reviewNote: 9,133 reviews, the highest volume in the research pass; a separate Yelp listing shows 575

evidence.line: Named gluten-free items exist, but an allergy-tracking source states it is not a dedicated gluten-free facility and warns of fryer cross-contact.

rating.reviewNote: Restaurant Guru shows 4.8 with 4,070 reviews, two sources broadly agreeing
